quality.rationale: Requires multi-step Levi-Civita contraction and 4-velocity normalization to derive the spin-angular momentum relation from tensor definitions.

origin.question: Problem 11.5.(b) In this case show that the angular momentum ("intrinsic angular momentum") can be found from the spin vector as$$\mathbf {J}_{\mathbf {(C . M .)}}^{\alpha \beta} \equiv \mathsf {S}^{\alpha \beta} = - \varepsilon^{\alpha \beta \gamma \delta} \mathsf {S}_{\gamma} \mathsf {u}_{\delta} \mathrm {.}$$
origin.solution:
(b) From the definition of $S_{\delta}$ :$$- \varepsilon^{\alpha  eta \gamma \delta} \mathbf {S}_{\gamma} \mathbf {u}_{\delta} =  rac {1}{2} \varepsilon^{\alpha  eta \gamma \delta} \varepsilon_{\gamma \mu 
u \sigma} \mathbf {J}^{\mu 
u} \mathbf {u}^{\sigma} \mathbf {u}_{\delta}.$$Now perform the summation over $\gamma$ [see Problems 3.27, 3.28]:$$-  rac {1}{2} \delta_{\mu 
u \sigma}^{\alpha  eta \delta} \mathbf {J}^{\mu 
u} \mathbf {u}^{\sigma} \mathbf {u}_{\delta} = - \mathbf {J}^{\alpha  eta} \mathbf {u}^{\delta} \mathbf {u}_{\delta} = \mathbf {J}^{\alpha  eta} \equiv \mathsf {S}^{\alpha  eta}.$$[Only the $\mathbf{J}^{\alpha  eta}$ terms are kept in the sum; by part (a) we cannot have a $\delta$ index on $\mathbf{J}$ due to the presence of $\mathfrak{u}_{\delta}$ .]
